title: Borderless Fullscreen
description: Makes Minecraft's fullscreen mode more configurable
body:
# Info
This mod changes Minecraft's fullscreen mode and makes it more customizable.
By default, it makes the game not minimize on lost focus, and allows other windows on top of the game.

## Features
- **New:** Capture the mouse cursor inside the game window to prevent accidentally clicking something on a different monitor. (Not available in 1.20.1 due to old GLFW version)

- Switch between exclusive fullscreen and borderless fullscreen mode without having to restart the game

- Prevent the game from automatically minimizing when tabbing out

- Prevent the game from starting in fullscreen mode

- Open the configuration screen by pressing CTRL and F11 (or what your fullscreen key is set to), or through [Mod Menu](https://modrinth.com/mod/modmenu)

## Troubleshooting
**On Windows 10 and 11**, Fullscreen Optimizations are enabled by default. Fullscreen Optimizations make the game run better, but they prevent anything from being on top of the game _while the game is focused_. If you want to, for example, have a picture-in-picture video on top of the game, or use the discord overlay, you have to **disable Fullscreen Optimizations.**

If you are on **Linux**, and the taskbar is above the game, **disable the Borderless Fullscreen option**.

**On some Linux distributions** you cannot have anything in front of the game, because some window systems don't support regular always-on-top windows. In that case, you have to create a custom window rule to put something in front of the game. [Here is an example for firefox picture-in-picture videos](https://github.com/Bestsoft101/Better-Fullscreen/issues/14#issuecomment-3348045964)

## Screenshots
![Screenshot of the configuration screen](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/c746ac579014c4b2e014279a3c884f9e46a62316_0.webp)
The config menu as of version 2.3.0

![Screenshot of the resolution selector](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/e5af1553bfb3d064f141cab87039067a4ee39a25_0.webp)
Choose which monitor and resolution the game should use when running in fullscreen
